meta.title: Jaguar AJ133 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Jaguar AJ133 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar AJ133 is a 5.0-litre V8 petrol — a superb engine let down by early timing‑chain issues. Backed by 330+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [JAG‑VERIFIED].

rows.metric: Confidence: 90% — based on 330 UK enquiries and cross‑referenced specialist rebuild reports.

rows.value: A mix of cheap fixes (coolant pipes, supercharger coupler) and expensive ones (timing chain)

rows.metric: Well‑supported; parts available from multiple UK suppliers

rows.value: Reconditioned cost against lowest host value (~£8,000) exceeds 75% threshold

rows.metric: Sum of the five rows above
rows.value: One-line verdict: The AJ133 is a superb V8 when maintained — but early timing‑chain tensioners fail without warning before 100k — buy 2013+ or with tensioner history.

rows.metric: Best for: Enthusiasts who value V8 character and can afford preventative maintenance.
rows.value: Avoid if: Budget buyers looking at pre‑2013 cars with no chain history.
Also powers the Land Rover Range Rover, Sport and Discovery → [link].
Labour estimate: Fitting an AJ133 typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG‑QUOTE].

rows.metric: Shared‑cost note: Parts cost is identical across Jaguar and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access.
rows.value: → Compare AJ133 engine prices from UK specialists
How much does a Jaguar AJ133 engine replacement cost?

rows.metric: A reconditioned AJ133 typically costs £5,500–£9,000 supply only [JAG‑QUOTE], varying by specification and whether it's naturally aspirated or supercharged. Fitting adds £910–£1,170 depending on labour rates.
rows.value: Is the Jaguar AJ133 a reliable engine?

rows.metric: The AJ133 scores 3.5/5 for reliability [JAG‑VERIFIED], with 90% confidence based on 330 UK enquiries and specialist reports. Post‑2013 units are much more robust.
rows.value: What is the most common AJ133 failure?

rows.metric: The most common AJ133 failure is timing chain tensioner wear on pre‑2013 engines, typically appearing between 60,000–120,000 miles.
rows.value: Which Jaguar models use the AJ133?

rows.metric: The AJ133 powers the Jaguar XF, XJ, F‑Type, XK and XFR — a range of executive saloons, grand tourers and sports cars.
rows.value: Is the Jaguar AJ133 the same as Land Rover's version?

rows.metric: Yes — it's the same 5.0‑litre V8 engine, used across both brands with identical parts and shared failure patterns.
rows.value: Why UK AJ133 Owners Trust jaguarengine.uk

rows.metric: * Every garage and engine rebuilder in our network is vetted, rated and reviewed by real AJ133 owners, as part of the Engine Finders network.
rows.value: * We compare real companies, not just lead‑gen sites — every specialist on EnginesMarket has genuine UK workshop premises and verified AJ133 experience.

rows.metric: * Our data is sourced from 330+ real UK AJ133 enquiries in 2025, combined with direct interviews with Jaguar specialists across the country.
rows.value: Find the best Jaguar AJ133 engine replacement UK drivers rely on. Compare real prices from trusted specialists on EnginesMarket today and get back on the road with confidence.

rows.metric: CTA Button: Compare Jaguar AJ133 Engine Prices Now →
rows.value: Internal note: Step 0 mode = Shared (AJ133 also fitted to Land Rover models). Cross‑link to Land Rover equivalent page to be added once built.
